summary: Drop `workflow archive` from .printing-press.json novel_features for the same reason `sync` was dropped — empty resource list, silent no-op on Etherpad's RPC-shaped API.
reason: Greptile P1 on PR #396: `channel_workflow.go` declares `resources := []string{}`, so `etherpad-pp-cli workflow archive` loops over zero resources, prints "Archived 0 items across 0 resources", and exits 0. Identical in behavior to the `sync` stub that was dropped from `novel_features` in patches[4]. The root cause is the same — every Etherpad list endpoint returns scalar IDs (`{"data":{"padIDs":[...]}}`) rather than hydratable objects, and `workflow archive` is built on the same sync machinery.
validated_outcome: novel_features now lists only the three commands that work end-to-end against Etherpad's API shape: tail, import, agent-context. channel_workflow.go carries an inline `// PATCH(drop-workflow-archive-novel-feature)` comment explaining why the resource list is empty so the next regen reviewer doesn't try to remove the comment as dead code.
upstream_issue: Same upstream root cause as patches[4] (drop-sync-and-analytics-novel-features) — generator should detect scalar-ID-list APIs and either skip the sync-derived workflow features or emit a per-resource fan-out adapter.
